jokes.text: 'CAUSE AT 8:00 YOU CAN'T SAY, "JANE, YOU IGNORANT SLUT."
jokes.setup: Sam asks why Saturday Night Live is on so late
jokes.punchline: Dad explains it's because of the famous SNL catchphrase that can't air in primetime
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-reference that shows Harold's surprising familiarity with edgy comedy. Great economy and character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Bill sleeping through the SNL opening while friends try to wake him
jokes.setup: Everyone's excited for Saturday Night Live and trying to wake Bill up
jokes.punchline: Bill is completely dead to the world despite increasingly frantic attempts
jokes.explanation: Classic setup of the heavy sleeper friend. Works for character but predictable.

jokes.text: WHY DO YOU GUYS ALWAYS PUT STUFF ON ME?
jokes.setup: Bill finally wakes up to find ice cream placed on him
jokes.punchline: His resigned complaint suggests this happens regularly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that implies an ongoing dynamic between the friends.

jokes.text: YOUR DAD'S THE COOLEST. / I HEARD THAT!
jokes.setup: Neal compliments Harold to Sam
jokes.punchline: Harold's immediate response from the kitchen shows he was listening
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective dad humor. Harold's timing and presence work well.

jokes.text: I THINK IF HE DID, HE'D DIE OF BLOOD LOSS.
jokes.setup: Someone suggests the acne-covered trombone player should pop his pimples
jokes.punchline: Kim's dark observation about the potential consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kim line - dark, cutting, and delivered with her characteristic bluntness. Great economy.

jokes.text: HEY! HEY, YOUR TUBA IS SO BIG AND SEXY! IT MAKES ME HOT LISTENING TO IT, BABY!
jokes.setup: The group is mocking band members
jokes.punchline: Daniel's over-the-top sexual harassment of the tuba player
jokes.explanation: Typical Daniel juvenile behavior but feels more cruel than funny in retrospect.

jokes.text: THAT'S NOT PLAYING AN INSTRUMENT. IT'S LIKE BLOWING INTO A TOILET.
jokes.setup: Lindsay defends the tuba player's musical ability
jokes.punchline: Daniel's crude comparison of tuba playing to toilet activities
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective visual comparison. Very Daniel-like dismissal of anything he doesn't understand.

jokes.text: LINDSAY, HERE'S AN IDEA. HOW ABOUT YOU BREAK UP OUR BAND SO YOU CAN MAKE OUT WITH NICK? OH, WAIT, YOU ALREADY DID THAT, THAT'S RIGHT.
jokes.setup: Lindsay defends the band members
jokes.punchline: Daniel's bitter sarcastic attack on Lindsay for her role in breaking up their band
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment that reveals Daniel's ongoing resentment. Well-structured sarcastic fake suggestion.

jokes.text: SHE HAD A TOOTH EMBEDDED IN THE ROOF OF HER MOUTH AND MY DAD HAD TO SUCTION OUT, LIKE, A GALLON OF BLOOD.
jokes.setup: Neal is telling a story about his dad's dental work during dinner
jokes.punchline: The grotesquely graphic details shared at mealtime
jokes.explanation: Perfect Neal - completely oblivious to social appropriateness. The dinner table timing makes it worse/better.

jokes.text: WELL, WHAT A CHARMING STORY.
jokes.setup: Neal finishes his graphic dental surgery story
jokes.punchline: Harold's perfectly dry, sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan dad response. Economy is flawless - says everything in three words.

jokes.text: YOU KNOW, THE WELFARE ROLLS ARE FULL OF VIDEO GAME PLAYERS. / NO, THEY'RE NOT. / WELL, THEY'RE GONNA BE. TRUST ME.
jokes.setup: Harold dismisses Sam's request for an Atari
jokes.punchline: Harold's ridiculous prediction that video games will create welfare dependents
jokes.explanation: Brilliant period-accurate parental paranoia. Harold's confidence in his absurd prediction is perfect. Highly quotable and prescient.

jokes.text: DON'T EVEN TRY IT.
jokes.setup: Sam watches Lindsay leave dinner early
jokes.punchline: Harold immediately shuts down Sam's attempt to follow suit
jokes.explanation: Perfect dad timing and awareness. Shows Harold knows his kids' tricks.

jokes.text: I THINK I'M GONNA GO PEE.
jokes.setup: Awkward moment with Lindsay and Nick at the diner
jokes.punchline: Daniel's blunt, inappropriate announcement to escape the tension
jokes.explanation: Typical Daniel crude exit from uncomfortable situations. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: JUST BE A BITCH. / I DON'T KNOW-- / LOOK, THIS IS FOR HIS OWN GOOD.
jokes.setup: Lindsay doesn't want to be mean to Nick
jokes.punchline: Kim's matter-of-fact advice to be cruel for kindness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kim philosophy - brutal honesty disguised as helpfulness. Her twisted logic is very character-specific.

jokes.text: IT'S LIKE THE TORTOISE AND THE HARE, ALL RIGHT? LITTLE RABBIT GETS TIRED, GUESS WHO WINS?
jokes.setup: Daniel explains his strategy for dealing with Kim's arguments
jokes.punchline: His mangled metaphor comparing relationship dynamics to the fable
jokes.explanation: Great Daniel moment - taking a classic fable and applying it to his dysfunctional relationship strategy.

jokes.text: ARE WE STILL TALKING ABOUT THE SAME THING?
jokes.setup: Daniel's tortoise and hare relationship metaphor gets convoluted
jokes.punchline: Ken's confused reaction to Daniel's rambling analogy
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response to Daniel's nonsensical advice. Ken as voice of reason.

jokes.text: EVERY TIME I SWING THAT RACKET, EVERYTHING JIGGLES.
jokes.setup: Neal's mom complaining about her tennis game
jokes.punchline: Her blunt admission about the physical realities of aging and exercise
jokes.explanation: Honest middle-aged woman humor about body changes. Relatable and specific.

jokes.text: THE ONLY THING I'M MISSING IS THE LITTLE HAT AND THE MINI-BAR IN THE BACK OF THE BUICK.
jokes.setup: Neal's mom complains about being Neal's chauffeur
jokes.punchline: Her specific imagery of what would complete her chauffeur role
jokes.explanation: Specific visual humor about parental martyrdom. The Buick detail makes it period-perfect.

jokes.text: BABY ANGEL.
jokes.setup: Neal's mom greets him after the adult conversation
jokes.punchline: Her embarrassingly affectionate pet name for Neal
jokes.explanation: Classic overbearing mother behavior. Neal's mortification is the real joke here.

jokes.text: CIAO. HA HA!
jokes.setup: Neal's mom says 'Ciao' as she leaves
jokes.punchline: Harold's mocking laugh at her pretentious goodbye
jokes.explanation: Harold's working-class disdain for pretension. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: WELL, LOOKS LIKE YOU ATE 'EM. / OHH.
jokes.setup: Ken asks Amy where the rest of the Lonely Hearts Club Band is
jokes.punchline: Amy's comeback that Ken ate the other band members
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that turns Ken's insult back on him. Amy shows she can hold her own verbally.

jokes.text: WHEN ARE THE TWINS DUE?
jokes.setup: Ken continues making weight-related jokes
jokes.punchline: Amy fires back with a pregnancy joke about Ken's belly
jokes.explanation: Standard but effective fat joke reversal. Shows Amy can dish it back.

jokes.text: NO, HE'S JUST A PAIN IN MY ASS.
jokes.setup: Ken asks if this 'bonehead' is Lindsay's friend
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's blunt dismissal of Ken
jokes.explanation: Lindsay's frustration with Ken boiling over. Direct and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: WELL, YOU KNOW WHAT? I'LL BET HE WOULDN'T SAY THAT IF HE WAS THE ONE DOING THE COOKING, RIGHT? HA HA. MOO.
jokes.setup: Jean mentions her husband thinks microwaved food can't be good
jokes.punchline: The salesman's sexist comment followed by his cow sound
jokes.explanation: Period-accurate casual sexism from pushy salesman. The 'moo' is weird and uncomfortable.

jokes.text: [CAPTAIN KIRK VOICE] WELL, SCOTTY, I HOPE YOU'RE NOT PLANNING TO COOK A TRIBBLE, BECAUSE I BELIEVE THEY'RE BETTER BARBECUED.
jokes.setup: Sam and Dr. Schweiber run into each other at the electronics store
jokes.punchline: Dr. Schweiber's elaborate Star Trek reference about microwaving
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Schweiber - awkward dad trying too hard to be cool with pop culture references. The voice impression adds character depth.

jokes.text: NOT REALLY FUNNY AT ALL.
jokes.setup: Dr. Schweiber claims running into Carol is 'funny'
jokes.punchline: His immediate correction that it's not funny at all
jokes.explanation: Great awkward moment showing Dr. Schweiber's guilt and panic. His immediate backtrack reveals his anxiety.

jokes.text: DON'T LEAVE ME HANGIN', NOW.
jokes.setup: Dr. Schweiber asks Sam to keep the Atari secret
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at a cool handshake/fist bump
jokes.explanation: Classic middle-aged man trying to be hip with the kids. Dr. Schweiber's awkwardness is perfectly pitched.

jokes.text: MAN, WHY DO I NEED TO GO TO A LASER SHOW WHEN I CAN MAKE MY OWN, IN MY MIND?
jokes.setup: Nick tries to convince everyone to go to Laser Floyd
jokes.punchline: Daniel's pseudo-philosophical dismissal of laser shows
jokes.explanation: Perfect Daniel pretentiousness. His fake deep philosophy about laser shows is beautifully absurd and quotable.

jokes.text: YEAH, ESPECIALLY IF YOU HAVE YOUR HAND UP WENDY FRANKLIN'S SHIRT.
jokes.setup: Nick describes Laser Dome as a metaphysical experience
jokes.punchline: Daniel's crude reference to his sexual encounter there
jokes.explanation: Typical Daniel crudeness that serves the plot by reminding everyone of his infidelity. Character-appropriate but not clever.

jokes.text: IS IT JUST ME OR DID IT GET A LITTLE CHILLY IN HERE?
jokes.setup: The group's mood turns cold after Daniel's Wendy Franklin comment
jokes.punchline: Nick's observation about the temperature change
jokes.explanation: Good awareness from Nick about the social dynamics. Shows he's more perceptive than he appears.

jokes.text: WATCH HER RUN, JUST LIKE A LITTLE RABBIT. I AM THE TORTOISE.
jokes.setup: Lindsay leaves abruptly for food
jokes.punchline: Daniel applying his tortoise and hare relationship theory
jokes.explanation: Great callback that shows Daniel actually believes his own ridiculous philosophy. The visual of him as tortoise is perfect.

jokes.text: SO, I WAKE UP THIS MORNING, AND GUESS WHAT IS SITTING ON THE END OF MY BED? / A TURD?
jokes.setup: Neal sets up a surprise reveal about what was on his bed
jokes.punchline: Bill's immediate assumption it was excrement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill response - immediately jumping to the grossest possibility. Great subversion of Neal's excited setup.

jokes.text: A TURD? YES, BILL, A TURD. AN ATARI VIDEO SET.
jokes.setup: Neal saying something was sitting on his bed
jokes.punchline: Bill's crude guess followed by the reveal it was actually an Atari
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection joke. Bill's logical but crude guess contrasts perfectly with the actual pleasant surprise.

jokes.text: WELL, THERE'S A LOT OF DIFFERENT KINDS OF HUGGING. I MEAN, WAS HE PATTING HER ON THE BACK WHILE HE DID IT, LIKE GUYS DO?
jokes.setup: Sam describes seeing Dr. Schweiber hugging another woman
jokes.punchline: Bill's technical analysis of hug types and male back-patting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bill logic - trying to scientifically categorize hugs. His observation about male hugging patterns is spot-on.

jokes.text: BILL, I'M QUITE SECURE WITH MY MANLINESS. IT'S OK TO HUG YOUR FRIENDS, SAM.
jokes.setup: Sam refuses to demonstrate the hug
jokes.punchline: Bill's overly confident assertion about his masculinity and friendship
jokes.explanation: Great Bill moment showing his attempt at mature masculinity. The overstatement makes it funny.

jokes.text: REMEMBER THAT TIME IN SCIENCE CLASS WHEN I TRIED TO SNEAK OUT A FART BUT IT CAME OUT A POOP AND THEN I HAD TO FLUSH MY UNDIES DOWN THE TOILET?
jokes.setup: Bill argues that friends should share all secrets
jokes.punchline: His graphic example of an embarrassing secret he shared
jokes.explanation: Peak Bill oversharing. His logic that this humiliating story equals relationship secrets is perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: WE'VE BEEN WAITING SINCE THE THIRD GRADE FOR YOU TO LIKE SOMEBODY. IT'S GREAT.
jokes.setup: Ken embarrassed about his crush
jokes.punchline: Daniel's revelation about how long Ken has been romantically inactive
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that reveals Ken's romantic inexperience while showing Daniel's genuine friendship.

jokes.text: YOU THINK SHE HAS A BOYFRIEND? / NOT EVEN AN EX. SHE'S A TUBA PLAYER.
jokes.setup: Ken asks about Amy's relationship status
jokes.punchline: Daniel's harsh assessment based solely on her instrument
jokes.explanation: Classic Daniel cruel logic. The idea that instrument choice determines romantic history is absurdly specific.

jokes.text: OH, HEY, DON'T FORGET TO LIFT THE SEAT THIS TIME OR MY MOM WILL KILL YOU.
jokes.setup: Bill goes to the bathroom at Neal's house
jokes.punchline: Neal's specific warning about toilet etiquette
jokes.explanation: Shows Neal's uptight household and his mother's control. Character-appropriate but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: YOU DON'T EVEN HAVE A DAD. / I DO TOO! I TALKED TO HIM 3 MONTHS AGO.
jokes.setup: Neal dismisses Bill's opinion in anger
jokes.punchline: Bill's defensive response about his absent father
jokes.explanation: Dark moment that reveals Bill's family situation. His defensive response about a 3-month gap shows the pain beneath.

jokes.text: HEY, GUYS, I'M SO LONELY. / WE HAVE TO GO. / CAN'T YOU HANG AROUND? WE COULD HAVE, LIKE, A LITTLE ASTEROIDS CONTEST, OR, UH, BENNY HILL.
jokes.setup: Sam and Bill want to leave after the awkward revelation
jokes.punchline: Neal's desperate attempts to get them to stay, including Benny Hill
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking Neal moment - his world is falling apart and he's trying to hold onto normalcy with desperate bribes.

jokes.text: CAN I GO TO THE BATHROOM?
jokes.setup: Kim interrogates Lindsay about Daniel and Wendy Franklin
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's escape request from the uncomfortable conversation
jokes.explanation: Classic escape move from uncomfortable social situation. Lindsay's desperation is palpable.

jokes.text: WHY DO PEOPLE HAVE AFFAIRS?
jokes.setup: Sam processing what he's learned about Dr. Schweiber
jokes.punchline: His innocent question to Lindsay about adult relationships
jokes.explanation: Sam's innocent confusion about adult behavior. His directness contrasts with the adults' deception.

jokes.text: NO, BACK. BACK. BAD TONGUE.
jokes.setup: Dr. Schweiber is examining Sam's mouth
jokes.punchline: His treatment of Sam's tongue like a misbehaving pet
jokes.explanation: Funny dentist humor - treating body parts like pets. Dr. Schweiber's casual authority is perfect.

jokes.text: IT'S JUST-- IT GETS SO HARD, SAM. I FEEL THAT THERE'S SOMETHING MISSING IN MY LIFE
jokes.setup: Dr. Schweiber is confessing his marital problems to Sam
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate emotional dumping on a child
jokes.explanation: Deeply uncomfortable and inappropriate. The comedy comes from the boundary violation - using a kid as a therapist.

jokes.text: IT'S JUST-- IT GETS SO HARD, SAM. I FEEL THAT THERE'S SOMETHING MISSING IN MY LIFE
jokes.setup: Dr. Schweiber trying to justify his affair to a child
jokes.punchline: The deeply inappropriate oversharing to a patient who's also a kid
jokes.explanation: Peak inappropriate adult behavior. The cringe comes from Dr. Schweiber treating Sam like a therapist rather than a child patient.

jokes.text: I SAW THIS DONAHUE ONCE ABOUT HUSBANDS THAT CHEAT. YOU CAN'T DO IT AT HOME, RIGHT? SO THEY ALL HAD SECRET LOVE NESTS.
jokes.setup: The boys are trying to figure out the garage door opener
jokes.punchline: Bill's TV-informed expertise about adultery logistics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill - getting his worldly knowledge from daytime TV. 'Secret love nests' is an amazing phrase from a 12-year-old.

jokes.text: I FEEL ODD.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asks if Ken really likes Amy
jokes.punchline: Ken's understated response to having romantic feelings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ken understatement. His discomfort with emotions expressed in the most minimal way possible.

jokes.text: I'M GONNA SAY, HEY, LADY, YOU WANT TO MESS WITH THE SCHWEIBER FAMILY? IS THAT WHAT YOU WANT? OK--BOOM! / HEAD BUTT!
jokes.setup: Neal explains his plan if they find the other woman
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous confrontation scenario ending with Bill's violent addition
jokes.explanation: Good Neal fantasy bravado with Bill's enthusiastic violence. Shows how kids imagine handling adult problems.

jokes.text: DON'T YOU JUST WANT TO REACH OUT AND TOUCH THEM?
jokes.setup: Amy compliments Ken's sideburns
jokes.punchline: Her unexpectedly forward comment about wanting to touch them
jokes.explanation: Amy being unexpectedly flirty and forward. Shows she's more confident than Ken assumed.

jokes.text: I'D BE HAPPY JUST TO GET A WIFE. BUT I DON'T THINK I WANT THE KIND THAT'S GONNA GET OLD AND FAT.
jokes.setup: Discussion about marriage and fidelity
jokes.punchline: Bill's shallow but honest admission about his preferences
jokes.explanation: Bill's typical teenage male perspective on relationships. His honesty about shallow preferences is both funny and realistic.

jokes.text: YOU KNOW WHAT WOULD BE COOL, GUYS? TO FIND A GIRL IN A BOTTLE, LIKE--LIKE I DREAM OF JEANNIE. I'D LIKE TO MAKE OUT WITH HER ON THAT LITTLE COUCH.
jokes.setup: Boys discussing relationships and marriage
jokes.punchline: Bill's specific fantasy about I Dream of Jeannie's furniture
jokes.explanation: Peak Bill sexual fantasy - focusing on the specific furniture detail. His innocent specificity makes it hilarious.

jokes.text: TO FIND A GIRL IN A BOTTLE, LIKE--LIKE I DREAM OF JEANNIE. I'D LIKE TO MAKE OUT WITH HER ON THAT LITTLE COUCH.
jokes.setup: Discussing ideal girlfriends
jokes.punchline: Bill's specific fantasy about Jeannie including the couch detail
jokes.explanation: Bill's very specific fantasy shows his attention to detail and innocent teenage sexuality. The couch detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: ELIZABETH... I'M COMIN' TO JOIN YOU, HONEY.
jokes.setup: Bill gets scared while investigating houses
jokes.punchline: His Sanford and Son reference about having a heart attack
jokes.explanation: Great Bill pop culture reference. His dramatic death scene impression shows his TV knowledge.

jokes.text: YOU ARE SEEING IT FOR FREE. I PAID FOR YOUR TICKET. / YEAH, A WHOLE $2.00. HOW YOU GONNA LIVE WITHOUT IT?
jokes.setup: Amy complains about the laser show cost
jokes.punchline: Her sarcastic response about Ken's $2 sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Amy showing her sarcastic side. Good period detail about 1980 pricing and teenage economics.

jokes.text: YEAH. NEXT TIME, YOU SHOULD BRING YOUR TUBA AND...JAM.
jokes.setup: Amy says the song rocks
jokes.punchline: Ken's awkward attempt to connect her tuba to rock music
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ken awkwardness - trying to be supportive but saying something ridiculous. The pause before 'jam' is everything.

jokes.text: BOY, YOU MUST'VE BEEN REALLY CUTE IN FIFTH GRADE...WITH YOUR LITTLE BLOND PIGTAILS.
jokes.setup: Kim mentioning she hasn't been to Laser Dome since fifth grade
jokes.punchline: Daniel's teasing about her childhood appearance
jokes.explanation: Daniel's gentle teasing of Kim shows their relationship dynamic. The specific image of pigtails adds charm.

jokes.text: DID YOU LEAN YOUR HEAD BACK WITH THAT SLUT?
jokes.setup: Daniel helps Kim adjust her seat at the laser show
jokes.punchline: Kim's jealous question about his past encounter with Wendy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kim jealousy - turning a romantic gesture into an interrogation about his infidelity.

jokes.text: THAT'S NEXT WEEK, MAN. TONIGHT'S SOUTHERN ROCK NIGHT. YEE-HAW!
jokes.setup: Everyone expects Pink Floyd laser show
jokes.punchline: It's actually Southern rock night with enthusiastic yee-haw
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection payoff. The enthusiastic 'yee-haw' from the employee makes it funnier.

jokes.text: LIKE, SHOULD I JUST SAY, YOU KNOW / YEAH. THAT'S PERFECT. / YEAH? / WHAT ARE YOU, A FREAKING 'MO?
jokes.setup: Ken asks for kissing advice
jokes.punchline: Daniel's sarcastic response calling him gay for asking
jokes.explanation: Period-accurate teenage homophobia but not particularly clever. Shows Daniel's typical cruel response to vulnerability.

jokes.text: I JUST... DIDN'T WANT TO... LEAD YOU ON, AND I KNOW IT'S SO STUPID, BUT... I THOUGHT THAT YOU MIGHT STILL LIKE ME. WOW. YOU'RE REALLY CONCEITED.
jokes.setup: Lindsay explaining her behavior to Nick
jokes.punchline: Nick's unexpected response calling her conceited
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of expectations. Lindsay's self-deprecating honesty gets met with Nick's blunt assessment of her assumptions.

jokes.text: WOW. YOU'RE REALLY CONCEITED.
jokes.setup: Lindsay confesses she thought Nick might still like her
jokes.punchline: Nick's understated response to her assumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nick deadpan response. His matter-of-fact delivery makes Lindsay's self-absorption clear.

jokes.text: I WANT TO KISS YOU. / UM... / OK.
jokes.setup: Amy asks why Ken is staring at her
jokes.punchline: Ken's blunt honesty followed by Amy's surprised acceptance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of romantic comedy tropes. Ken's direct honesty works better than any smooth line would have.

jokes.text: YEAH. MAYBE YOU CAN TEACH ME ABOUT THOSE SPACE INVADERS. HUH? / OH... / [SOBBING] DAD.
jokes.setup: Harold surprises Sam with an Atari and wants to bond
jokes.punchline: Sam breaks down crying from the emotional weight of his secret
jokes.explanation: Perfect emotional payoff. Harold's sweet gesture becomes devastating because Sam knows what he knows about Dr. Schweiber.

jokes.text: BOY. MUST'VE REALLY WANTED THAT GAME, HUH?
jokes.setup: Sam breaks down crying
jokes.punchline: Harold's completely wrong interpretation of Sam's tears
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking dramatic irony. Harold's innocent misunderstanding while Sam carries the weight of adult secrets.
